trajectory.description: Observation: First training run (CLIP ViT-L/14) took 5068s (~84 min) for 2 epochs, val_macro_f1=0.0319 at epoch 0, dropped to 0.0037 at epoch 1. Analysis: Very low F1 — likely the WeightedRandomSampler is too aggressive (sqrt inverse-freq oversamples rare classes that have only a few samples, causing overfitting on rare classes and degrading common-class performance; also LR may be too high after one epoch). Training one CLIP-L epoch at 224 takes ~40min. With 6.6h left, I need to be efficient. Decision: Switch to CLIP ViT-B/16 (smaller, faster), use standard sampling (no weighted), with class-balanced loss instead. Submit eval first with current best.pt to validate pipeline, then retrain better.

Decision: First submit current best.pt to baseline the eval pipeline while planning a better training run.

trajectory.description: Observation: First training (CLIP-L, 2 epochs, weighted sqrt sampler) gave val_macro_f1 0.032 which collapsed to 0.004 epoch 2; eval gave 0.027. Pipeline works. ~84min/epoch is too slow. 6.5h left, 27 iters. Analysis: Need (1) faster training - switch to CLIP ViT-B/16 (3-4x faster) at 224, (2) drop weighted sampling (caused collapse), use standard CE with optional logit-adjusted loss for long-tail, (3) lower LR, cosine schedule, freeze early layers or use lower backbone LR. With ViT-B/16 multi-GPU at bs=256, expect ~20min/epoch, can do 4-5 epochs in ~80min. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use CLIP ViT-B/16, standard sampling, AdamW with low backbone LR, cosine schedule, label smoothing, train 4 epochs, then submit.

trajectory.description: Observation: 10 epochs CLIP-B/16 plateaued around val_macro_f1=0.091 (eval=0.064). Training F1 isn't improving past epoch 7. Analysis: The model is plateauing — likely need (1) higher resolution or (2) larger model. With 5h left, ~6min/epoch for B/16 at 224. CLIP-L/14 was ~40min/epoch at 224. Better strategy: continue B/16 longer with class-balanced strategy or switch to higher resolution. Actually the eval F1 (0.064) is much lower than val (0.091) — there's a domain gap. Key issue: long-tailed classes need more representation. Let me try (1) higher resolution 336 with CLIP-B/16, OR (2) use a logit-adjusted loss to handle long-tail. Actually let me try training CLIP-L/14 at 224 with better hyperparameters — given time constraints, 4 epochs at ~40min = 2.7h. With 5h left that's feasible. Decision: Train CLIP-L/14 with cosine schedule, mild logit adjustment for long-tail, 3-4 epochs. First, let me modify train.py to add logit-adjusted loss and switch to L/14.

Observation: CLIP-L/14 training failed - val_macro_f1=0.004 after 3 epochs, model not learning. Took 67min. Analysis: Something went wrong with the L/14 run - perhaps the lr was too low or logit-adjustment broke training. Best previous result: B/16 with val_f1=0.091, eval=0.064. With 3.9h left and 23 iters, I should fall back to the working B/16 setup but try to improve it. Key improvements to try: (1) higher resolution (336 if possible with B/16), (2) more aggressive training with the original recipe that worked. Let me check what's in train.py and revert to the working B/16 config but train longer + at higher res. Decision: First check current train.py contents fully, then run a B/16 setup that we know works at higher res or for more epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 7 (CLIP-L/14) failed - val_f1=0.004. Likely the logit adjustment broke training or LR was wrong. Best.pt was overwritten with this bad model. I have 3.9h left, 22 iters. Iter 6's best.pt was 0.091 but is now overwritten. Analysis: Need to retrain CLIP-B/16 (which worked) with the original recipe — no logit adjustment. Per epoch ~6min, can do 12-15 epochs in ~75-90min. Better idea: train at higher resolution (384 with B/16 since CLIP-B/16 has positional embeddings at 224, would need interpolation). Stick with 224 but train longer with better augmentation + careful LR schedule. Decision: Rewrite train.py with the recipe that worked at iter 5 (CLIP-B/16, no logit adj, lr=1e-5, head_lr=1e-4, label smoothing 0.1, cosine), train 12 epochs from scratch.
